questions.text: Which Terraform version introduced the native test framework?
questions.explanation: The native Terraform test framework with .tftest.hcl files was introduced as stable in Terraform 1.6. Provider mocking was added in 1.7. Prior to 1.6, teams relied on third-party tools like Terratest (Go) or Kitchen-Terraform (Ruby) for integration testing.

questions.text: What file extension do Terraform test files use?
questions.explanation: Terraform test files use the .tftest.hcl extension. They are typically placed in a tests/ subdirectory or alongside the configuration they test. Terraform discovers all .tftest.hcl files in the current directory and its tests/ subdirectory.

questions.text: What are the two valid values for 'command' in a run block?
questions.explanation: Run blocks support command = plan (performs plan only, no infrastructure created, faster and safer) or command = apply (creates real infrastructure, allows testing actual outputs and behavior). command defaults to apply if not specified.

questions.text: What does 'mock_provider' do in a test file?

options.text: Creates a fake provider for local development
Replaces a real provider with a mock that returns configured default values
Runs tests against a mock API endpoint

questions.explanation: mock_provider replaces a provider with a mock implementation during tests. Mock resources and data sources return configured default values instead of making real API calls. This enables fast unit-level testing without cloud credentials or incurring costs.

questions.text: What happens to infrastructure created by 'command = apply' in tests after the test completes?

options.text: It is automatically destroyed by Terraform after each run block
It is moved to a test workspace
It is left running and must be manually destroyed

questions.explanation: Terraform test automatically destroys infrastructure created by apply run blocks after the test (or run block) completes, ensuring clean test environments. If a test fails mid-way, Terraform still attempts cleanup. Use -no-cleanup flag to keep infrastructure for debugging.

questions.text: How do you specify different variable values for a specific run block vs. the whole test file?

options.text: Use -var flag on each run block
Add a variables {} block inside the run block (overrides file-level variables)
Create separate .tfvars files per run block
Run blocks always inherit file-level variables only

questions.explanation: Variables can be set at two levels: at the file level (applies to all runs) and inside individual run blocks (overrides file-level for that run). This allows testing different scenarios with different inputs in the same test file.

questions.text: What is the assert block's 'condition' evaluated as?
questions.explanation: The condition in an assert block is any Terraform expression that evaluates to a boolean. You can reference outputs, resource attributes, and use Terraform functions: condition = length(output.ids) > 0, condition = output.name != "", etc. If false, the test fails with error_message.

questions.text: Which third-party testing tool was commonly used before Terraform 1.6?
questions.explanation: Terratest (by Gruntwork, written in Go) was the dominant integration testing framework before native testing. It deploys real infrastructure, makes assertions with Go's testing package, then destroys everything. The native framework provides a simpler alternative, though Terratest still excels for complex scenarios.

questions.text: Can assert blocks reference resources from a previous run block in the same test file?

options.text: No — each run block is isolated
Yes — state accumulates across run blocks in the same test file
Only if using the same provider
Only with command = apply, not plan

questions.explanation: State accumulates across run blocks within a test file. A later run block can reference resources created by an earlier run block. This allows testing sequences of operations — create, update, verify. The state is only destroyed at the end of the test file, not between run blocks.

questions.text: How do you run only tests matching a specific filter?
questions.explanation: terraform test -filter=<pattern> runs only test run blocks whose names match the pattern. This is useful for running a single test during development. You can also specify a file path: terraform test -filter=tests/vpc.tftest.hcl to run only tests in that file.
